scenes.summary: The opening credits roll over a montage of Elle getting ready and riding her bike to her sorority house.
Elle reads a card from Warner and gets excited about their upcoming date.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Receives a Romantic Card
content_desc.summary_paragraph: In her vibrant pink bedroom, Elle Woods is getting ready when her Chihuahua, Bruiser, brings her a pink envelope. She opens the card to find a sweet message wishing her luck and celebrating her relationship with Warner. Touched by the romantic gesture, Elle smiles warmly. She then blows a kiss to a framed photograph of her boyfriend, anticipating their evening together.

key_actions: Dog carrying a card in its mouth
Blowing a kiss to a photograph
dialogue_highlights: Bruiser, what's this?
Good luck tonight. Elle and Warner forever.

scenes.summary: Elle tells her sorority sisters that she thinks Warner is going to propose to her tonight.

content_desc.clip_title: Sorority Sisters Discuss Impending Engagement
content_desc.summary_paragraph: The scene opens on a framed photograph of a young man in a pink room before panning out to a lively outdoor courtyard party. Three sorority sisters excitedly discuss the strong possibility that one of them will be proposed to that evening. They speculate about the size of the diamond ring and squeal with joy. The clip ends with the girls rushing off to find the perfect outfit for the occasion.

dialogue_highlights: I can't believe you're getting engaged!
It's not like she'd FedEx a 6-carat diamond.
You guys have to help me pick out the perfect outfit.

trivia.text: The production settled on having Elle go to a fictional college called CULA.
trivia.match_reasoning: Margot is wearing a teal and orange shirt with the letters "CULA" printed on it, the fictional college the characters attend.

scenes.summary: Elle shops for the perfect dress for her date and outsmarts a saleswoman trying to trick her.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Outsmarts a Deceitful Boutique Saleswoman
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods and her friends are shopping at a high-end boutique for the perfect dress for her upcoming date, where she expects a proposal. A condescending saleswoman attempts to take advantage of Elle, assuming she is just a 'dumb blonde' with a credit card, by trying to sell an old dress at full price. However, Elle uses her expert knowledge of fabrics and fashion history to quickly expose the lie. Triumphant, Elle and her friends celebrate her impending engagement.

themes_and_concepts: Don't judge a book by its cover
dialogue_highlights: There's nothing I love more than a dumb blonde with Daddy's plastic.
It's impossible to use a half-loop top-stitching on low-viscosity rayon. It would snag the fabric.
So if you're trying to sell it to me for full price, you picked the wrong girl.

scenes.summary: Warner picks Elle up for their date, and she descends the stairs looking beautiful.

content_desc.clip_title: Warner Picks Up Elle for a Romantic Date
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Warner arrives at the sorority house to pick up Elle for a highly anticipated date. After being let in, he waits at the bottom of the stairs as Elle makes a grand entrance in a stunning pink dress. They exchange compliments and a kiss while her sorority sisters watch in excitement. Before leaving, her friends give her a quick spritz of perfume.

dialogue_highlights: Wow. You look so beautiful.
So do you.

scenes.summary: At a fancy restaurant, Warner breaks up with Elle because he needs to marry someone more serious for his political career.

content_desc.clip_title: Warner Breaks Up With Elle at Dinner
content_desc.summary_paragraph: During a romantic dinner, Elle anticipates a marriage proposal from Warner. Instead, he breaks up with her to pursue his serious political ambitions at Harvard Law. Devastated by his claim that he needs a 'Jackie, not a Marilyn,' Elle cries hysterically and storms out.

dialogue_highlights: I need to marry a Jackie, not a Marilyn.
So you're breaking up with me because I'm too blonde?

scenes.summary: Elle cries outside the restaurant and in the car as Warner tries to justify his decision and drops her off.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Gets Dumped by Warner Outside a Restaurant
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Following a devastating breakup dinner, a tearful Elle Woods refuses to get into Warner's car. Warner attempts to justify his decision by stating he needs someone serious for his future, implying Elle is not. Elle defends her background, but ultimately yields and gets into the car when Warner points out the wet ground will ruin her shoes.

key_actions: Refusing to get in the car
dialogue_highlights: I told you. I need someone serious.
But I'm seriously in love with you. Isn't that enough?
You're gonna ruin your shoes.

scenes.summary: Elle spends days depressed in her room, eating chocolates and watching soap operas, while her friends try to comfort her.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Mourns Her Breakup in Bed
content_desc.summary_paragraph: A sorority sister knocks on Elle's door for makeup advice, but is informed by another sister that Elle was recently dumped. Inside her pink bedroom, a heartbroken Elle lies in bed eating chocolates and yelling at a soap opera on TV. Her two best friends enter with large sodas, determined to cheer her up and get her out of her week-long slump.

setting.location: Sorority house hallway and Elle's bedroom

key_actions: Throwing a chocolate at the TV
dialogue_highlights: Oh, it's terrible. He dumped her.
Liar!
What's the one thing that always makes us feel better no matter what?
sound_design: Soap opera dialogue and dramatic music

trivia.text: The production settled on having Elle go to a fictional college called CULA.
trivia.match_reasoning: A poster bearing the logo for the fictional college 'CULA' is visible on the bulletin board outside Elle's room.

scenes.summary: At the nail salon, Elle sees a magazine article about Warner's brother's fiancee, a law student, and gets an idea.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods' Epiphany at the Nail Salon
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods is deeply depressed after being dumped, leading her friends to gossip about her unkempt appearance while they get manicures. While sitting in the salon reading a magazine, Elle spots an engagement announcement for her ex-boyfriend's brother. She realizes his fiancee is a law student, sparking a sudden epiphany. Elle declares that she must become a law student to be taken seriously and win her ex back, abruptly leaving the salon.

dialogue_highlights: This is the type of girl that Warner wants to marry! This is what I need to become to be serious!
What? Practically deformed?
No... A law student.

scenes.summary: Elle tells her parents she wants to go to Harvard Law School to win Warner back.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Announces Her Law School Plans
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods rests at the edge of her luxurious backyard pool while telling her parents about her plan to attend Harvard Law School. Her goal is to win back her ex-boyfriend, but her parents are baffled by the idea. Her mother reminds her of her beauty pageant success, while her father dismisses law school as a place for 'boring, ugly, and serious' people, traits he insists Elle does not possess.

key_actions: Resting on the edge of a swimming pool
dialogue_highlights: Law school is for people who are boring and ugly and serious. And you, button, are none of those things.
Going to Harvard is the only way I'm going to get the love of my life back.
Honey, you were first runner-up at the Miss Hawaiian Tropic contest. Why are you gonna throw that all away?

scenes.summary: Elle meets with her academic advisor, who is skeptical about her chances of getting into Harvard Law School.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Plans for Harvard Law
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods meets with her academic advisor to discuss her ambitious plan to attend Harvard Law School. The advisor expresses strong skepticism, noting Elle's background in Fashion Merchandising and outlining the rigorous requirements for admission. Unfazed by the daunting academic hurdles, Elle remains supremely confident in her abilities, humorously comparing the challenge to her past sorority experiences.

dialogue_highlights: I don't need back-ups. I'm going to Harvard.
Harvard won't be impressed that you aced 'History of Polka Dots.'
I once had to judge a tighty-whitey contest for Lambda Kappa Pi. Trust me, I can handle anything.

trivia.text: The production settled on having Elle go to a fictional college called CULA.
trivia.match_reasoning: The fictional college name 'CULA' is clearly visible on the mug sitting on the academic advisor's desk.

scenes.summary: Margot and Serena find Elle studying for the LSATs instead of going on vacation. They are shocked to learn she wants to go to law school to win Warner back.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Plans for Harvard Law
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods is found sitting on her bedroom floor surrounded by LSAT prep books. When her friends Margot and Serena walk in, they are confused by her studying. Elle reveals her grand plan to attend Harvard Law School to win back her ex-boyfriend, Warner, and her friends offer their unique brand of support.

dialogue_highlights: The LSATs are an exam. Girls, I'm going to Harvard!
Once Warner sees me as a serious law student, he'll totally want me back.
My lucky scrunchy. It helped me pass Spanish.

scenes.summary: Elle presents her video essay for Harvard admissions, showcasing her leadership skills and ability to use legal jargon in everyday situations.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Harvard Video Essay Introduction
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods introduces herself in a video essay for Harvard Law School while floating in a luxurious swimming pool. To demonstrate her leadership skills, the video cuts to her presiding over a sorority meeting. She commands the room's attention to address a pressing issue: the maintenance staff switching their toilet paper from Charmin to a generic brand.

setting.location: Swimming pool and sorority house dining room

dialogue_highlights: My name is Elle Woods, and for my admissions essay, I'm gonna tell all of you at Harvard why I'm gonna make an amazing lawyer.
All those opposed to chafing, please say 'aye.'

trivia.text: "And we'd have re-thinks and re-writes, often in the middle of the night." An unused idea for the finished film included having a cameo appearance of Judge Judy during Elle's Harvard video essay in which Elle and her friends chased down the show's host, but the scene was cut when Judge Judy Sheindlin could not get on board.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip shows Elle Woods presenting her Harvard video essay, which the fact explicitly names as the scene where an unused Judge Judy cameo was originally planned.

scenes.summary: Elle studies intensely for the LSATs, skipping social events. She takes practice tests and her scores steadily improve.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Studies for the LSAT and Films Admissions Video
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods studies diligently for the LSAT in a library, trying to ignore a noisy fraternity party outside. The scene transitions to her Harvard Law admissions video, where she floats in a pool and demonstrates her impressive memory by recounting a soap opera plot. Later, she takes a practice LSAT at her sorority house, looking disappointed when she scores a 143.

dialogue_highlights: I'm able to recall hundreds of important details at the drop of a hat.
Why, yes, Margot, I do. Once again we join Hope in the search for her identity.

scenes.summary: Elle takes the LSAT exam alongside other students.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Arrives for the LSAT Exam
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods struts confidently across a sunny college campus in a bright pink halter dress, drawing stares and a wolf whistle, to which she playfully replies with legal jargon. The scene abruptly shifts to a quiet, tense lecture hall where a sign reads 'L.S.A.T. Exam.' Elle sits among other students, her confident demeanor replaced by a look of nervous anticipation as the proctor prepares to start the test.

key_actions: Waiting for an exam to begin
dialogue_highlights: I feel comfortable using legal jargon in everyday life.
I object!

scenes.summary: Elle receives her LSAT score of 179. She and her sorority sisters celebrate her success.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Celebrates Her LSAT Score
content_desc.summary_paragraph: A sorority sister rushes into the house carrying the mail, excitedly announcing that the LSAT scores have arrived. She hands an envelope to Elle Woods, who nervously opens it while surrounded by her supportive sisters. Upon seeing her score of 179, Elle screams in joy, and the entire sorority erupts into a massive celebration with confetti and cheering.

dialogue_highlights: The LSAT scores! It's here!

scenes.summary: The Harvard admissions committee reviews Elle's video essay and application. Despite some skepticism, they decide to admit her for diversity.

content_desc.clip_title: Harvard Admissions Committee Accepts Elle Woods
content_desc.summary_paragraph: A group of serious Harvard Law School admissions officers watch Elle Woods' unconventional video essay on a small television. Despite her background as a fashion major and her bubbly presentation in a bikini, they are impressed by her perfect grades and high LSAT score. Finding humorous ways to justify her unique extracurriculars, the committee ultimately decides to admit her to the prestigious university.

characters: Elle Woods (on video, enthusiastic and confident)
Head Admissions Officer (skeptical but ultimately convinced)
Admissions Committee Members (serious, analytical, slightly amused)
dialogue_highlights: And that's why you should vote for me, Elle Woods, future lawyer for the class of 2004.
She also designed a line of faux fur panties for her sorority's charity project.
Elle Woods... Welcome to Harvard.
sound_design: Upbeat pop music from the video

trivia.text: "And we'd have re-thinks and re-writes, often in the middle of the night." An unused idea for the finished film included having a cameo appearance of Judge Judy during Elle's Harvard video essay in which Elle and her friends chased down the show's host, but the scene was cut when Judge Judy Sheindlin could not get on board.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip shows the admissions committee watching Elle's Harvard video essay, which this fact notes originally included a cameo by Judge Judy.

trivia.text: The production settled on having Elle go to a fictional college called CULA.
trivia.match_reasoning: An admissions officer explicitly states that Elle has a 4.0 GPA from "CULA," the fictional college mentioned in the fact.

scenes.summary: Elle arrives in Boston and drives to the Harvard campus. She moves into her dorm with the help of movers.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Arrives at Harvard
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods drives her black Porsche convertible across a bridge and onto the Harvard Law School campus, accompanied by her chihuahua, Bruiser. A large moving truck follows her to her new dorm, Wyeth House. Dressed in a striking pink leather outfit, she immediately stands out among the conservatively dressed students, who stare and make comments as she cheerfully gives her dog bottled Evian water and directs the movers.

characters: Elle Woods (excited, cheerful, out of place)

setting.location: Harvard Law School campus, Cambridge, Massachusetts

key_actions: Pouring bottled water for a dog
dialogue_highlights: This is our new house for the next three years.
Hey, Brad, check out Malibu Barbie!
Where's the beach, honey?

trivia.text: "We met at a hotel on Sunset Boulevard and discussed the film ... we were both concerned about some aspects, like how can the audience feel sorry for a rich girl driving a Porsche; and she had to dress in a very particular way that wasn't distracting or off-putting ...
trivia.match_reasoning: Elle is seen driving her black Porsche convertible as she arrives at college in this clip.

trivia.text: Although the film was primarily set at Harvard University, campus scenes were filmed at USC, University of California, Los Angeles, California Institute of Technology, and Rose City High School in Pasadena, California.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip shows Elle arriving at the Harvard University campus, with a sign for 'Harvard Law School' clearly visible.

scenes.summary: Elle attends orientation and meets her new classmates, who have very different backgrounds from her. She looks for Warner but doesn't find him.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Arrives at Harvard Law Orientation
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods arrives at Harvard Law School orientation, immediately standing out in her bright pink outfit. At the registration table, she confuses the staff by asking for a social events calendar. Later, during an icebreaker circle on the lawn, her highly academic classmates share their impressive intellectual achievements. When it is Elle's turn, she cheerfully introduces herself and her dog Bruiser, highlighting her fashion degree and sorority background.

key_actions: Sitting in a circle on the grass
dialogue_highlights: My social events calendar is missing.
You know, maybe you should check with the cruise director on the lido deck.
Hi. I'm Elle Woods and this is Bruiser Woods, and we're both Gemini vegetarians.

trivia.text: The cast and crew also did a lot of research, with McCullah and Smith visiting the Stanford Law School for a week during orientation time; a scene of a group composed of new students going around in a circle was inspired on law students the screenwriters eavesdropped during their visit.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip features the specific scene where the new law students sit in a circle on the grass and take turns introducing themselves.

trivia.text: The production settled on having Elle go to a fictional college called CULA.
trivia.match_reasoning: During her introduction to the group, Elle explicitly states that she has a bachelor's degree from CULA.

scenes.summary: Elle gets ready for her first day of class, putting on her glasses and checking her outfit in the mirror.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Prepares for Her First Law Class
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods stands in front of a mirror, admiring her new, serious outfit for law school. She asks her dog, Bruiser, to wish her luck on her first day as a serious law student. After putting on a pair of glasses, she confidently declares that she looks the part, while Bruiser whines and hides under a blanket.

dialogue_highlights: Wish me luck, Bruiser. This is my first class as a serious law student.
I totally look the part.

scenes.summary: Elle walks confidently down the hallway and runs into Warner. He is shocked to see her at Harvard Law School.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Surprises Warner at Harvard Law
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods confidently struts down the halls of Harvard Law School, drawing stares and whispers from other students. She casually bumps into her ex-boyfriend, Warner, who is utterly shocked to see her there. When he incredulously asks if she got into Harvard Law, she delivers her iconic line, 'What, like it's hard?' before cheerfully walking off to class.

dialogue_highlights: You got into Harvard Law?
What, like it's hard?

scenes.summary: Elle attends her first class with the strict Professor Stromwell. She is called on but is unprepared, leading Stromwell to ask Vivian if Elle should be kicked out of class.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods is Kicked Out of First Law Class
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Professor Stromwell sets a strict and intimidating tone on the first day of law school. After questioning a male student about a quote on the chalkboard, she calls on Elle Woods, who admits she is unprepared for the reading assignment. Stromwell then asks Vivian Kensington if Elle should be dismissed, and upon Vivian's agreement, Elle is forced to leave the classroom.

dialogue_highlights: The law is reason free from passion.
Um, actually, I wasn't aware that we had an assignment.
Would you support my decision to ask her to leave class and to return only when she is prepared?

trivia.text: For the role of Warner's new girlfriend Vivian, Smith suggested casting Chloë Sevigny.
trivia.match_reasoning: The character Vivian, who is called on by the professor in this scene, was originally considered to be played by Chloë Sevigny.

trivia.text: This suggestion did not work out, so Selma Blair was cast instead; Blair and Witherspoon had previously been together in Cruel Intentions, allowing their friendship to be an anchor between their characters.
trivia.match_reasoning: Selma Blair appears in this scene as Vivian, who agrees with the professor that Elle should be asked to leave the class.

scenes.summary: Emmett comforts Elle outside the classroom and gives her advice on how to handle her professors. Warner approaches, and Vivian arrives shortly after, introducing herself to Elle as Warner's fiancee.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Discovers Warner is Engaged to Vivian
content_desc.summary_paragraph: After being kicked out of Professor Stromwell's class, a frustrated Elle sits on a bench and receives helpful advice from Emmett about surviving her professors. Her mood brightens when she spots Warner, but the reunion quickly turns awkward. Warner introduces her to Vivian Kensington, who smugly reveals that she is Warner's new fiancee, leaving Elle in absolute shock.

dialogue_highlights: I'm his fiancee.
Uh... I'm sorry. I just hallucinated.
Pooh Bear, I didn't know she would be here.

trivia.text: This suggestion did not work out, so Selma Blair was cast instead; Blair and Witherspoon had previously been together in Cruel Intentions, allowing their friendship to be an anchor between their characters.
trivia.match_reasoning: Selma Blair (Vivian) and Reese Witherspoon (Elle) appear together and interact in this scene.

trivia.text: The screenwriters envisioned Luke Wilson as they were coming up with Elle's love interest Emmett Richmond.
trivia.match_reasoning: Luke Wilson appears in this scene as Emmett Richmond, who gives Elle advice.

scenes.summary: A devastated Elle drives erratically through the streets, crying, before pulling up to a beauty salon.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Frantically Drives to a Beauty Salon
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods is seen driving her black Porsche convertible frantically down a city street. She looks distressed and appears to be urgently searching for something. Suddenly, she spots 'Neptune's Beauty Nook', abruptly pulls over, and rushes out of her car, expressing immense relief.

setting.location: City street, outside Neptune's Beauty Nook

dialogue_highlights: Oh, thank God!

trivia.text: "We met at a hotel on Sunset Boulevard and discussed the film ... we were both concerned about some aspects, like how can the audience feel sorry for a rich girl driving a Porsche; and she had to dress in a very particular way that wasn't distracting or off-putting ...
trivia.match_reasoning: Elle is visibly driving a Porsche in this scene.

scenes.summary: Elle confides in Paulette about her breakup and Vivian. Paulette shares her own heartbreak story and encourages Elle to fight to get Warner back.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Confides in Paulette at the Nail Salon
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods enters a nail salon in tears, seeking comfort from manicurist Paulette. She laments that her ex-boyfriend Warner is engaged to another woman despite her following him to Harvard. Paulette relates by sharing her own tragic breakup story involving her stolen dog. Inspired by their shared heartbreak, Paulette urges Elle to fight to win Warner back.

dialogue_highlights: I just wish I had never gone to Harvard.
Steal the bastard back.

trivia.text: And every decision came from a certain innocence [of the character]." Jennifer Coolidge was cast as Elle's manicurist friend Paulette, a role which Courtney Love and Kathy Najimy had also been considered for, according to some rumors Coolidge heard.
trivia.match_reasoning: Jennifer Coolidge is shown in this scene playing Paulette, who is acting as Elle's manicurist.

scenes.summary: Professor Callahan introduces his highly coveted internship to the class. He questions Elle and Vivian, and Elle gives a bold answer that catches his attention.

content_desc.clip_title: Professor Callahan Questions Elle Woods in Law Class
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Professor Callahan announces four highly coveted internship spots at his firm, setting a fiercely competitive tone for his law class. He begins his 'usual torture' by questioning Elle Woods on legal terminology, to which she provides a naive answer that amuses the rest of the students. Callahan then turns to Vivian Kensington, who delivers a textbook response. Unfazed by the embarrassment, Elle raises her hand to change her answer, confidently stating she prefers a challenge.

dialogue_highlights: Let the bloodbath begin.
I would rather have a client who's innocent.
I'd pick the dangerous one 'cause I'm not afraid of a challenge.

scenes.summary: Warner plays football with his friends in the park. Elle studies nearby in a bikini and fur coat, making sure Warner notices her.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Distracts the Football Game
content_desc.summary_paragraph: A group of young men are playing a game of touch football on a grassy campus lawn. Their game is abruptly halted when Elle Woods arrives, dressed in a bright pink fluffy jacket and a sparkly bikini top. She sets up a red lounge chair, sits down with her dog, and begins to read, completely distracting the players, including her ex-boyfriend Warner.

dialogue_highlights: Whoa, bro. Who is that?
Warner, you know her?
Hi.

scenes.summary: Elle tries to join Warner and Vivian's study group in the library, bringing snacks. Vivian coldly rejects her, and Elle leaves.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Rejected by Study Group
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods approaches a study group in the law library, offering a basket of food and asking to join. Vivian and Warner coldly reject her, claiming it's a 'smart people thing' and that the group is full. After another student insults her, Elle defends herself with a sharp retort before walking away.

characters: Elle Woods (hopeful, then hurt and defensive)
dialogue_highlights: No. It's, like, a smart people thing.
You know, if you had come to a rush party, I would have at least been nice to you.
You must have heard it from Vivian.

scenes.summary: Elle talks to Margot on the phone from her dorm room. Margot excitedly announces her engagement while trying on wedding dresses.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Calls Her Friends From Law School
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Feeling lonely in her Harvard dorm room, Elle Woods calls her best friends back home for comfort. She reaches Margot, who is excitedly trying on a wedding dress with Serena. Her friends are completely oblivious to Elle's melancholy state, chattering about engagements and new haircuts instead. The call ends abruptly when Margot accidentally rips the wedding dress.

setting.location: Harvard dorm room and a bridal boutique

dialogue_highlights: I miss you guys, too. The people here are so vile.
Oh, my God! I almost forgot to tell you! I got bangs! My hair is so now.
Keep June 1st open. You're one of my bridesmaids, and give Warner our love, because I'm getting married!

scenes.summary: Vivian and Enid are talking in the hallway when Elle opens her door. They invite her to an upcoming party, tricking her into thinking it's a costume party.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Gets Invited to a Costume Party
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods overhears Vivian and Enid inviting some classmates to a party. Eager to socialize, Elle steps out of her room and asks about the event. Vivian reluctantly invites her but deceitfully claims it is a costume party, hoping to embarrass Elle.

dialogue_highlights: Is somebody at this school actually having a party?
But it's a costume party. You probably wouldn't want to come.
I love costume parties.

trivia.text: This suggestion did not work out, so Selma Blair was cast instead; Blair and Witherspoon had previously been together in Cruel Intentions, allowing their friendship to be an anchor between their characters.
trivia.match_reasoning: Selma Blair and Reese Witherspoon, who play Vivian and Elle respectively, are seen interacting in this scene.

scenes.summary: Elle arrives at the party dressed as a Playboy bunny, quickly realizing she was tricked. She confronts Warner, who tells her she is not smart enough to get the internship.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Attends a Party in a Bunny Costume
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods arrives at a law school gathering dressed in a revealing Playboy bunny costume, quickly realizing she was tricked into thinking it was a costume party. Despite the humiliation, she confidently confronts Vivian and her friends with a witty comeback. She then speaks with her ex-boyfriend Warner, who condescendingly tells her she isn't smart enough for a prestigious internship, leading Elle to a heartbreaking realization about how he truly views her.

dialogue_highlights: Except when I dress up as a frigid bitch, I try not to look so constipated.
You're not smart enough, sweetie.
I'm never gonna be good enough for you, am I?

trivia.text: The woman who was leading it spent three years at Stanford trying to change the name 'semester' to 'ovester.' I started laughing and I realized everyone in the room took it very seriously.
trivia.match_reasoning: Enid is shown in the clip petitioning to change the word 'semester' to 'ovester', a detail directly inspired by the real-life event described in the fact.

scenes.summary: A montage begins showing Elle studying hard, buying a new laptop, and walking confidently on campus.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods' Determined Exit and New Purchase
content_desc.summary_paragraph: After a frustrating encounter at a costume party, Elle Woods turns her back on Warner and storms out, loudly declaring her worth. Still wearing her pink bunny costume, she heads straight to a computer store to gear up for law school. She confidently carries a large iMac box past a line of customers, including Emmett, who watches her with quiet amusement.

setting.location: College party, then a computer store

key_actions: Walking past a line of people
dialogue_highlights: I'll show you how valuable Elle Woods can be!
Don't ask.
Wasn't gonna.

scenes.summary: Elle ignores Warner when he tries to help her reach a book in the library.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Studies Hard at Harvard
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods is shown diligently typing an assignment on her bright orange laptop in her dorm room. She then strides confidently across the Harvard campus carrying a heavy stack of law books, drawing surprised looks and whispers from her peers. Later, in the law library, a male student assists her in reaching a book from a high shelf.

key_actions: Reaching for a book on a high shelf
dialogue_highlights: I love that sweater. It's Chanel.
Was she carrying books?
Thanks.

scenes.summary: Elle confidently answers a question in Professor Stromwell's class.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Answers in Law Class
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods is called upon by Professor Stromwell during a rigorous law school lecture. Despite being caught slightly off guard, she successfully answers a question about the need for evidentiary support when filing a claim. The professor then challenges her to elaborate further, testing her understanding of the material.

characters: Elle Woods (attentive, slightly nervous but capable)
dialogue_highlights: So, you've filed a claim. What next? Ms. Woods?
Meaning you need reasonable belief that your claim should have, like, evidentiary support?

scenes.summary: Elle exercises on an elliptical machine in her dorm room while reading a textbook.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Studies While Exercising on an Elliptical
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Bruiser the Chihuahua sits on a bed, attentively watching a Taco Bell commercial on a CRT television. The camera pans to reveal Elle Woods vigorously working out on an elliptical machine. She is multitasking by intensely reading a large, thick textbook propped up on the machine's console.

key_actions: Reading a book while working out
dialogue_highlights: Yo Quiero Taco Bell?
Welcome back. Now, in the article, you talk about all sorts of things. Corrupt cops...

scenes.summary: Elle and Paulette study their textbooks while sitting under hair dryers at the salon.

content_desc.clip_title: Studying Law at the Hair Salon
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Two women sit under dome hair dryers in a brightly colored salon, both engrossed in reading. The blonde woman in the foreground is diligently studying a massive 'Civil Procedure' law book and taking notes. The camera pans to reveal a small Chihuahua sitting in the adjacent chair, also under a dryer and wearing a salon cape.

dialogue_highlights: ♪ Not gonna give up / Until I get what's mine

trivia.text: And every decision came from a certain innocence [of the character]." Jennifer Coolidge was cast as Elle's manicurist friend Paulette, a role which Courtney Love and Kathy Najimy had also been considered for, according to some rumors Coolidge heard.

scenes.summary: Elle answers a question in Professor Callahan's class, impressing him.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Answers Correctly in Law Class
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Professor Callahan writes 'Diminished Capacity' on the chalkboard and asks the class for its purpose. Elle Woods confidently raises her hand and provides the correct legal answer, surprising her classmates. The professor acknowledges her correct response, and an upbeat pop song begins to play as Elle smiles proudly.

dialogue_highlights: And the purpose of diminished capacity is?
To negate mens rea?

scenes.summary: Elle and Paulette confront Paulette's ex-boyfriend Dewey. Elle uses legal jargon to intimidate him into giving Paulette her dog back.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Helps Paulette Reclaim Her Dog
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle and Paulette arrive at a junkyard trailer park in Elle's Porsche to confront Paulette's ex-boyfriend, Dewey. When Paulette loses her nerve, Elle steps in and uses intimidating legal jargon to confuse him. Empowered by Elle's support, Paulette confidently demands her dog back. The two women drive away victorious with their pets.

dialogue_highlights: I'm Elle Woods, Miss Bonafonté's attorney.
I'm taking the dog, dumbass!

trivia.text: "We met at a hotel on Sunset Boulevard and discussed the film ... we were both concerned about some aspects, like how can the audience feel sorry for a rich girl driving a Porsche; and she had to dress in a very particular way that wasn't distracting or off-putting ...
trivia.match_reasoning: Elle is seen driving her Porsche at the beginning of the clip, a detail the director and actress discussed regarding her character's relatability.

trivia.text: And every decision came from a certain innocence [of the character]." Jennifer Coolidge was cast as Elle's manicurist friend Paulette, a role which Courtney Love and Kathy Najimy had also been considered for, according to some rumors Coolidge heard.
trivia.match_reasoning: Jennifer Coolidge appears throughout the scene playing the character of Paulette.

scenes.summary: In class, Elle brilliantly counters Warner's argument regarding a sperm donor case, winning the debate and impressing Professor Callahan.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Wins the Sperm Donor Argument
content_desc.summary_paragraph: In a Harvard Law classroom, Warner Huntington III makes a legal argument regarding a sperm donor's visitation rights, earning praise from Professor Callahan. Elle Woods raises her hand and delivers a brilliant, unconventional counter-argument about the donor's other 'emissions'. Her point amuses the class and impresses Callahan, who declares she has won her case, leaving Warner embarrassed.

dialogue_highlights: Now you're thinking like a lawyer.
Why now? Why this sperm?
I believe you've just won your case.

scenes.summary: Professor Callahan asks Elle to apply for his internship. She hands him a pink, scented resume, which he and Emmett discuss.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Submits Her Pink Scented Resume
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Professor Callahan stops Elle Woods after class to compliment her performance and suggests she apply for his internship. Elle enthusiastically agrees and hands him her resume, which is notably pink and scented. After she leaves, Callahan questions her seriousness, but Emmett defends her, noting her potential before smelling the unique resume himself.

dialogue_highlights: It's pink. Oh, and it's scented. I think it gives it a little something extra, don't you think?
Do you think she just woke up one morning and said, 'I think I'll go to law school today'?
Well, that lapse in judgment aside, I think she's got a lot of potential.

scenes.summary: Elle and Paulette sit in the salon, looking out the window at the snow and toasting with cans of soda.

content_desc.clip_title: Festive Toast at the Nail Salon
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Following a brief interaction between two men, the scene shifts to a snowy night outside a brightly lit nail salon. Inside, Elle and Paulette sit together amidst festive Christmas decorations. They share a warm smile and toast each other with canned drinks, enjoying a quiet moment of friendship.

setting.location: Nail salon interior viewed from outside

dialogue_highlights: No dialogue

trivia.text: And every decision came from a certain innocence [of the character]." Jennifer Coolidge was cast as Elle's manicurist friend Paulette, a role which Courtney Love and Kathy Najimy had also been considered for, according to some rumors Coolidge heard.

scenes.summary: Establishing shots of the Boston skyline.

content_desc.clip_title: Salon Chat to Harvard Campus Transition
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle and Paulette share a quiet moment drinking from mugs at the nail salon, which is decorated with string lights. The scene then smoothly crossfades to a sweeping daytime aerial shot of the Harvard University campus situated along the Charles River. This visual transition establishes a shift in location and time, moving the story back to the academic setting.

dialogue_highlights: What's going on?

scenes.summary: Students crowd around a notice board to see who got the internship. Elle is thrilled to find her name on the list and gloats to Warner.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Secures the Final Prestigious Law Internship
content_desc.summary_paragraph: A large group of anxious law students crowds around a hallway bulletin board to see who has been chosen for Professor Callahan's highly coveted internship. Vivian and Warner celebrate their selection, noting there is only one spot remaining. Elle Woods pushes her way to the front and is thrilled to discover her name on the list. She takes a moment to gloat to her ex-boyfriend Warner, comparing her triumph to a past romantic encounter, before triumphantly announcing she has shopping to do.

dialogue_highlights: This is so much better than that!
Excuse me. I have some shopping to do.
Four hours?

trivia.text: This suggestion did not work out, so Selma Blair was cast instead; Blair and Witherspoon had previously been together in Cruel Intentions, allowing their friendship to be an anchor between their characters.

scenes.summary: Professor Callahan briefs his new interns on the Brooke Windham murder case. Elle defends Brooke, arguing that exercise produces endorphins and happy people don't kill their husbands.

content_desc.clip_title: Law Firm Meeting on Brooke Windham Case
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods and her peers attend a law firm meeting led by Professor Callahan to discuss their new client, Brooke Windham. Brooke is a fitness mogul accused of murdering her wealthy husband. While the others suspect her guilt, Elle defends Brooke, famously arguing that her exercise routine produces endorphins, and happy people don't commit murder.

dialogue_highlights: Exercise gives you endorphins. Endorphins make you happy. Happy people just don't shoot their husbands. They just don't.

trivia.text: Ali Larter originally wanted to play one of Elle's sorority sisters, but upon reading the script, she fell in love with the character of Brooke Taylor Windham, the fitness instructor on trial for murder.

scenes.summary: The legal team interviews Brooke Windham in jail. Brooke refuses to provide an alibi, but she and Elle recognize each other as Delta Nu sorority sisters.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Meets Her Client Brooke in Jail
content_desc.summary_paragraph: A legal team, including law student Elle Woods, interviews their client, Brooke Taylor-Windham, in a jail meeting room. Brooke vehemently denies killing her older husband but stubbornly refuses to provide an alibi to her defense attorney, Professor Callahan. After the tense meeting concludes, Elle and Brooke recognize each other, bonding over their shared sorority and fitness background.

dialogue_highlights: Then show them a picture of his dick. That might clear a few things up.
I'm a Delta Nu, and I'm a huge fan of yours.
Well, thank God one of you has a brain.

scenes.summary: At the salon, Paulette is flustered when the handsome UPS guy arrives. Elle teaches her the "bend and snap" maneuver to get his attention.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Teaches Paulette the Bend and Snap
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle and Paulette are getting their nails done when the handsome UPS delivery man arrives. Paulette is too nervous to flirt with him, resulting in an awkward exchange. After he leaves, Elle decides to teach Paulette a foolproof flirting technique. She demonstrates the famous bend and snap maneuver to build Paulette's confidence.

dialogue_highlights: He's got a package.
It's called the bend and snap.

trivia.text: And every decision came from a certain innocence [of the character]." Jennifer Coolidge was cast as Elle's manicurist friend Paulette, a role which Courtney Love and Kathy Najimy had also been considered for, according to some rumors Coolidge heard.

trivia.text: Later, while brainstorming at a bar in Los Angeles, McCullah Lutz came up with a solution: "What if Elle shows [Paulette] a move so she can get the UPS guy?" On the spur of the moment, Smith invented a move, standing up and demonstrating what would become the bend and snap.

scenes.summary: Elle teaches Paulette and the other women in the nail salon how to do the 'bend and snap' to get a man's attention.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Teaches the Bend and Snap in a Salon
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods demonstrates her signature 'bend and snap' flirting technique to Paulette in a vibrant beauty salon. She encourages the other patrons and staff to join in, turning the lesson into an impromptu group dance routine. The scene culminates with everyone enthusiastically performing the move together, proving its universal appeal.

dialogue_highlights: Bend and snap!
A little less bend, a little more snap.
Oh, my God, the bend and snap! Works every time!

trivia.text: The "bend and snap" scene – where Elle explains to Paulette how to get her crush's attention – almost did not make it into the movie.

trivia.text: It was a completely drunken moment in a bar." Director Robert Luketic later adapted the "bend and snap" move into a dance number for the film.

scenes.summary: The legal team discusses the murder case over Chinese food, trying to figure out who killed Heyworth Windham.

content_desc.clip_title: Legal Team Debates Murder Suspects
content_desc.summary_paragraph: A legal team gathers in an office to discuss potential suspects in a murder case while eating Chinese takeout. They systematically rule out the victim's daughter and ex-wife based on a lack of financial motive and a solid alibi. Elle Woods maintains her strong belief in their client's innocence, but Professor Callahan insists that intuition isn't enough and they need a concrete alibi.

dialogue_highlights: Well, all I know is it's not Brooke.
That's touching, Elle. But we need an alibi.

scenes.summary: Elle visits Brooke in prison and learns that Brooke's alibi is that she was getting liposuction, a secret she refuses to reveal to protect her fitness empire.

content_desc.clip_title: Brooke Confesses Her Alibi to Elle
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle visits Brooke in prison, bringing her a basket of beauty necessities. Elle presses Brooke for her alibi, explaining that her legal team desperately needs it. Brooke tearfully confesses that she was getting liposuction, a secret that would ruin her fitness empire if revealed. Elle promises to keep her secret safe.

dialogue_highlights: I was getting liposuction.
I'd rather go to jail than lose my reputation.
Brooke, your secret's safe with me.

trivia.text: Ali Larter originally wanted to play one of Elle's sorority sisters, but upon reading the script, she fell in love with the character of Brooke Taylor Windham, the fitness instructor on trial for murder.
trivia.match_reasoning: Ali Larter appears in this scene as Brooke Taylor Windham, who is seen in prison and mentions her fitness video, "Brooke's Butt-Buster workout," matching the fact's description of her character.

scenes.summary: Back at the law firm, Elle refuses to tell Callahan Brooke's alibi, citing the bonds of sisterhood, which frustrates the team.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Refuses to Break the Bonds of Sisterhood
content_desc.summary_paragraph: During a team meeting at the law firm, Professor Callahan discovers Elle visited their client and obtained an alibi. However, Elle refuses to reveal the alibi, citing the sacred bonds of sorority sisterhood. Her refusal frustrates Callahan and baffles her peers, especially Warner, who urges her to be selfish for the sake of her career. Ultimately, Callahan assigns Elle and Emmett to interview the victim's ex-wife at a spa.

dialogue_highlights: Because I promised her I'd keep it a secret, and I can't break the bonds of sisterhood.
Screw sisterhood! This is a murder trial, not some scandal at the sorority house.
Who cares about Brooke? Think about yourself.

scenes.summary: Emmett and Elle drive to a spa in the Berkshires to interview Heyworth's ex-wife.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle and Emmett Argue in the Car
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Emmett and Elle are driving together, discussing their client Brooke. Emmett expresses his distrust of Brooke, believing she is hiding something and criticizing her profession. Elle defends Brooke and playfully calls Emmett a 'butt-head' for his lack of faith in people. Emmett is amused and surprised by the childish insult, leading to a lighthearted exchange.

dialogue_highlights: You know, you're really being a butt-head.
I mean, no one's called me a 'butt-head' since about the ninth grade.
Maybe not to your face.

trivia.text: Ali Larter originally wanted to play one of Elle's sorority sisters, but upon reading the script, she fell in love with the character of Brooke Taylor Windham, the fitness instructor on trial for murder.
trivia.match_reasoning: Elle and Emmett are discussing Brooke, the fitness instructor on trial for murder, and her career of telling women they are too fat.

trivia.match_reasoning: Luke Wilson is visible in the scene playing the character of Emmett Richmond.

scenes.summary: Emmett and Elle arrive at the spa.

content_desc.clip_title: Arriving at the Golden Springs Spa
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Emmett drives up to the Golden Springs Spa, marveling at the unfamiliar environment. He asks Elle how they will locate the person they are looking for. Elle explains she called ahead and the person is in the mud room, which makes Emmett nervously question if she will be naked.

dialogue_highlights: So this is what a spa looks like. Wow.
She's not, uh, naked, is she?

scenes.summary: Emmett and Elle interview Mrs. Windham Vandermark while she gets a mud mask, and she suggests Brooke was having an affair with the pool boy.

content_desc.clip_title: Questioning Mrs. Vandermark at the Spa
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle and Emmett track down Mrs. Windham Vandermark at a luxurious spa to question her about the murder of Hayworth. They find her relaxing on a spa bed, wearing a blue face mask and wrapped in gold foil. During the interview, Mrs. Vandermark shares scandalous gossip from her daughter regarding Brooke's relationship with Hayworth, ultimately hinting at an affair with a cabana boy.

dialogue_highlights: So I hear that that little tart from California shot poor Heyworth.
Well, she did say that they humped like gorillas.
Haven't you seen the cabana boy?

scenes.summary: Emmett and Elle walk out of the spa, with Elle expressing her belief that the ex-wife is lying.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Deduces a Lie from Hair Color
content_desc.summary_paragraph: The scene opens with a woman relaxing in a spa, wearing a blue face mask and a towel on her head. The scene then cuts to Elle Woods and Emmett Richmond walking down a park path carrying briefcases. Elle confidently tells Emmett that the woman is lying, using her 'icky brown' hair color as evidence.

characters: Woman in spa / Brooke Taylor Windham (relaxed)
dialogue_highlights: She's lying.
Did you see the icky brown color of her hair?

scenes.summary: Emmett drops Elle off at campus at night, encouraging her to use her power as a blonde for good.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle and Emmett Discuss the Power of Being Blonde
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle and Emmett walk together at night before getting into his car. They discuss the stereotypes and discrimination associated with hair color. Emmett encourages Elle to channel the power of being blonde towards the greater good. As Elle exits the car, they share a flirtatious exchange about Emmett going blonde.

setting.location: Campus street and inside a car

dialogue_highlights: You know, being a blonde is actually a pretty powerful thing.
How do you think I'd look as a blonde, you know?
I'm not sure you could handle it.

scenes.summary: Elle intervenes when a guy named David is rejected by a girl, pretending to be his heartbroken ex to boost his confidence.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Defends David Kidney from Mean Girls
content_desc.summary_paragraph: David Kidney approaches a girl on campus at night to ask her out, but she harshly rejects him, calling him a loser. Witnessing this, Elle Woods steps in to defend him. She pretends to be his heartbroken ex-lover, loudly confronting him about their passionate past to make the mean girls reconsider his social value. Her clever ruse works, leaving the girls impressed and David bewildered but grateful.

dialogue_highlights: Girls like me don't go out with losers like you.
Sorry for what? For breaking my heart, or for giving me the greatest pleasure I've ever known and then just taking it away?

scenes.summary: Vivian visits Elle in her room, and they bond over Elle's dog Bruiser and the revelation that Warner was wait-listed for Harvard.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle and Vivian Bond Over Warner
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Vivian enters Elle's room to retrieve a deposition and commends Elle for keeping a client's alibi a secret. The two women begin to bond, sharing a laugh over Warner's helplessness and the fact that he was wait-listed for law school. Their newfound friendship is solidified when Vivian holds and plays with Elle's dog, Bruiser.

dialogue_highlights: I still can't believe you didn't tell Callahan the alibi.
It's not my alibi to tell.
His father had to make a call.

scenes.summary: The trial begins, and Enrique the pool boy testifies that he was having an affair with Brooke, which Brooke denies to Elle.

content_desc.clip_title: Brooke Windham's Murder Trial Begins
content_desc.summary_paragraph: The high-profile murder trial of Brooke Windham begins with a media frenzy outside the courthouse. Inside, the prosecution calls the victim's daughter and ex-wife, who both testify against Brooke to paint her in a negative light. The pool boy, Enrique Salvatore, takes the stand and admits to having a sexual relationship with Brooke, while the prosecutor presents a blue thong as his required 'uniform.' After court adjourns, Brooke insists to Elle that she is innocent of the affair, claiming a Delta Nu would never sleep with a man who wears a thong.

dialogue_highlights: Have you and Mrs. Windham had sexual relations? Yes. Okay? Yes.
You know a Delta Nu would never sleep with a man who wears a thong.

scenes.summary: Paulette tries the 'bend and snap' on the UPS guy but accidentally breaks his nose.

content_desc.clip_title: Paulette Bumps Heads with the UPS Guy
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods looks shocked in a crowded courtroom before the scene cuts to Paulette in her nail salon. Paulette is applying makeup when the handsome UPS delivery man arrives with a package, leaving her visibly flustered. While trying to sign for the delivery, she drops her pen on the floor. They both bend down to retrieve it and accidentally bump heads, leaving Paulette stunned and holding her chest.

Can you sign?
I got it.

trivia.match_reasoning: Paulette attempts to use the move to get the attention of the UPS guy, who is delivering a package in the scene.

scenes.summary: Elle is on the phone with Paulette in the courthouse hallway. She notices Enrique's designer shoes and realizes he is gay.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Discovers Enrique's Secret
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods is on the phone in the courthouse hallway, comforting her friend Paulette about a dating mishap. After hanging up, she waits by the water fountain where Enrique is drinking. When she impatiently taps her foot, Enrique makes a snarky comment about her 'last-season Prada shoes,' leading Elle to a shocking realization that could change the course of the trial.

dialogue_highlights: Don't stomp your little last-season Prada shoes at me, honey.
These aren't last season.

scenes.summary: Elle tells her team that Enrique is gay, but Callahan dismisses her. Emmett takes over the cross-examination and tricks Enrique into revealing his boyfriend's name, causing an uproar in the courtroom.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Proves the Witness is Gay in Court
content_desc.summary_paragraph: During a recess, Elle Woods deduces that the key witness, Enrique, is gay based on his knowledge of designer shoes. Despite Professor Callahan's skepticism, Emmett decides to test Elle's theory on the stand. He cleverly tricks Enrique into revealing his boyfriend's name, causing an uproar in the courtroom. Enrique's boyfriend storms out, destroying Enrique's credibility and proving he lied about having an affair with the defendant.

dialogue_highlights: Gay men know designers. Straight men don't.
And your boyfriend's name is... Chuck.
You bitch!

scenes.summary: Elle walks down the hallway at the law firm and is told by Vivian that Callahan wants to see her.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle and Vivian's Hallway Encounter
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods walks down a busy, upscale law firm hallway carrying a stack of files. She runs into Vivian Kensington, who is struggling with a much larger pile of documents. Vivian informs Elle that their boss, Callahan, wants to see her before she leaves, adding a snide remark about him needing a doughnut. Elle cheerfully offers to help Vivian with her files, but Vivian declines, and Elle proceeds to Callahan's office.

dialogue_highlights: Callahan asked to see you before you leave.
He already has his coffee, but maybe he needs a doughnut.

scenes.summary: Callahan praises Elle's instincts but then makes an inappropriate sexual advance. Elle realizes he only hired her for her looks and leaves his office in tears, which Vivian witnesses.

content_desc.clip_title: Professor Callahan Inappropriately Propositions Elle Woods
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Professor Callahan calls Elle Woods into his office to praise her legal instincts and initiative on their current case. However, the conversation takes a dark turn when he places his hand on her leg and propositions her, revealing his true, predatory intentions. Elle, disgusted and disillusioned, calls him a pathetic asshole and storms out. Outside, Vivian misinterprets the situation and insults Elle, leaving her crying alone in the elevator.

characters: Elle Woods (initially pleased, then shocked, disgusted, and tearful)
dialogue_highlights: And I'm a law student who just realized her professor is a pathetic asshole.
Too bad. I thought you were a law student who wanted to be a lawyer!
Maybe you should sleep with the jury, too. Then we can win the case.

scenes.summary: Emmett stops Elle in the lobby as she is leaving. She tells him about Callahan's behavior and quits the internship, despite Emmett's pleas for her to stay.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Quits Law School After Harassment
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods exits an elevator in tears and is intercepted by Emmett. She tearfully confesses that she is quitting law school because Professor Callahan sexually harassed her, realizing he only hired her for her looks. Despite Emmett's encouragement to stay and prove herself, a disillusioned Elle walks out of the building to return to California.

dialogue_highlights: Callahan only gave me that internship because he liked the way I looked. Which he made clear tonight when he tried to feel me up.
What if you're trying to be somebody you are?
Call me if you're ever in California, okay?

trivia.text: The screenwriters envisioned Luke Wilson as they were coming up with Elle's love interest Emmett Richmond.
trivia.match_reasoning: Luke Wilson appears in this scene as Emmett Richmond, trying to convince Elle to stay.

trivia.text: Middleton desired to cast Paul Bettany as Emmett, but the crew felt that the character should be American whereas Bettany is British.
trivia.match_reasoning: The character of Emmett, who is featured prominently in this scene, was almost played by Paul Bettany.

scenes.summary: Elle goes to the nail salon to say goodbye to Paulette, feeling defeated. Professor Stromwell overhears and gives Elle a stern pep talk, convincing her not to give up.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Decides to Quit Law School
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods packs her belongings into her convertible, ready to leave Harvard Law School after a devastating encounter with her professor. She visits her friend Paulette at the salon to say a tearful goodbye, expressing her belief that no one will ever see past her looks. However, Professor Stromwell overhears the conversation and delivers a stern but empowering message, challenging Elle not to let one man ruin her life.

dialogue_highlights: I mean, all people see when they look at me is blond hair and big boobs. No one's ever gonna take me seriously.
Callahan never saw me as a lawyer, he just saw me as a piece of ass.
If you're going to let one stupid prick ruin your life, you're not the girl I thought you were.

trivia.text: "The world looks at Elle and sees someone who is blonde and beautiful but nothing more.
trivia.match_reasoning: Elle's dialogue about people only seeing her as "blonde hair and big boobs" and not taking her seriously directly mirrors this description of how the world perceives her.

trivia.text: And every decision came from a certain innocence [of the character]." Jennifer Coolidge was cast as Elle's manicurist friend Paulette, a role which Courtney Love and Kathy Najimy had also been considered for, according to some rumors Coolidge heard.
trivia.match_reasoning: Jennifer Coolidge appears in this scene as Elle's manicurist friend, Paulette.

scenes.summary: Emmett and Vivian visit Brooke in jail and inform her of Callahan's actions. Disgusted, Brooke fires Callahan and announces she has new representation.

content_desc.clip_title: Emmett Reveals Callahan's Harassment to Brooke and Vivian
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Emmett turns off a television broadcasting an interview with Callahan and sits down with Vivian to speak with their client, Brooke, in a prison visiting room. Brooke questions her trust in Callahan, prompting Vivian to make a snide remark about Elle. Emmett then reveals that Elle quit because Callahan hit on her. Vivian immediately realizes she misjudged Elle and expresses deep regret for her previous behavior.

dialogue_highlights: Callahan hit on her, so she quit.
I feel terrible. I made a huge mistake.
If he doesn't trust me, why should I trust him?

trivia.text: This suggestion did not work out, so Selma Blair was cast instead; Blair and Witherspoon had previously been together in Cruel Intentions, allowing their friendship to be an anchor between their characters.
trivia.match_reasoning: Selma Blair appears in this scene as Vivian.

trivia.text: Ali Larter originally wanted to play one of Elle's sorority sisters, but upon reading the script, she fell in love with the character of Brooke Taylor Windham, the fitness instructor on trial for murder.
trivia.match_reasoning: Ali Larter appears in this scene as Brooke Taylor Windham, who is seen here in jail.

trivia.text: The screenwriters envisioned Luke Wilson as they were coming up with Elle's love interest Emmett Richmond.
trivia.match_reasoning: Luke Wilson appears in this scene as Emmett Richmond.

scenes.summary: Brooke officially fires Callahan in court. Elle makes a grand entrance in a pink suit and takes over as Brooke's lead counsel, with Emmett supervising. Elle begins her cross-examination of Chutney Windham.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Takes Over the Murder Trial Defense
content_desc.summary_paragraph: In a dramatic courtroom twist, Brooke Windham fires her attorney, Professor Callahan, and appoints law student Elle Woods as her new legal representation. Elle successfully argues her right to practice under the supervision of Emmett Richmond, who volunteers for the role. After a brief, comedic interruption from her supportive friends, Elle begins her cross-examination of the key witness, Chutney Windham. Despite a rocky start, she focuses her questioning on the timeline of events surrounding the murder.

dialogue_highlights: You're fired. I have new representation.
Rule 3.03 of Supreme Judicial Court states that a law student may appear on behalf of a defendant in criminal proceedings.
I'll supervise, Your Honor.

scenes.summary: Elle cross-examines Chutney Windham, catching her in a lie about washing her hair after getting a perm. Chutney accidentally confesses to shooting her father, and the judge dismisses the case against Brooke.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Wins the Case Using Hair Care Knowledge
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Elle Woods cross-examines Chutney Windham in a tense courtroom scene. Using her expert knowledge of hair care and perm maintenance, Elle catches Chutney in a lie about being in the shower during her father's murder. Under pressure, Chutney breaks down and confesses to accidentally shooting her father while aiming for her stepmother. The judge dismisses the case against Brooke Windham, and Elle celebrates her triumphant legal victory.

dialogue_highlights: Because isn't it the first cardinal rule of perm maintenance that you're forbidden to wet your hair for at least 24 hours after getting a perm at the risk of deactivating the ammonium thioglycolate?
I didn't mean to shoot him. I thought it was you walking through the door!
The rules of hair care are simple and finite. Any Cosmo Girl would have known.

trivia.text: Ali Larter originally wanted to play one of Elle's sorority sisters, but upon reading the script, she fell in love with the character of Brooke Taylor Windham, the fitness instructor on trial for murder.
trivia.match_reasoning: Ali Larter is visible in the clip playing Brooke Taylor Windham, the fitness instructor on trial for murder.

trivia.text: While filming the courthouse scenes, Raquel Welch requested cinematographer Anthony B.
trivia.match_reasoning: Raquel Welch is visible in the gallery during this courthouse scene.

trivia.text: The film originally ended at the courthouse right after Woods won the case, with Elle on the courthouse steps sharing a victory kiss with Emmett, then cutting one year into the future to her and a now-blonde Vivian starting their own Blonde Legal Defense Club at law school.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip shows the conclusion of the trial where Woods wins the case and walks down the courthouse steps, which was the film's original ending point.

scenes.summary: Elle exits the courtroom victorious and is swarmed by reporters. Warner tries to win her back, but she rejects him, realizing she needs a partner who respects her. Emmett congratulates her.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Rejects Warner's Apology
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Following her triumphant victory in court, Elle Woods exits into a hallway swarming with reporters. Warner intercepts her, admitting he was wrong and confessing his love in an attempt to win her back. Realizing her own worth and potential, Elle swiftly rejects him, stating she needs a partner who isn't a 'bonehead,' and confidently walks away.

dialogue_highlights: And you are the girl for me.
But if I'm gonna be a partner in a law firm by the time I'm 30, I need a boyfriend who's not such a complete bonehead.

trivia.text: The film originally ended at the courthouse right after Woods won the case, with Elle on the courthouse steps sharing a victory kiss with Emmett, then cutting one year into the future to her and a now-blonde Vivian starting their own Blonde Legal Defense Club at law school.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip shows Elle at the courthouse immediately after winning her case, which is the exact narrative beat where the film's original ending took place.

scenes.summary: Two years later, Elle gives the valedictorian speech at her Harvard Law School graduation. On-screen text reveals the fates of the main characters, including Elle and Emmett's relationship.

content_desc.clip_title: Elle Woods Delivers Inspiring Harvard Law Graduation Speech
content_desc.summary_paragraph: Two years later, Elle Woods is chosen as the class-elected speaker for the Harvard Law School graduation. She delivers an inspiring speech about the importance of passion, courage, and self-belief in the practice of law and life. As she concludes, the graduates celebrate by throwing their caps in the air. Meanwhile, on-screen text reveals the happy fates of her friends and the downfall of her ex-boyfriend.

key_actions: Throwing graduation caps in the air
dialogue_highlights: I have come to find that passion is a key ingredient to the study and practice of law and of life.
You must always have faith in people, and most importantly, you must always have faith in yourself.
We did it!

trivia.text: So test audiences were saying, 'We want to see what happens — we want to see her succeed.' So that's why we rewrote for graduation".
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip shows the graduation scene, which was written as a new ending after test audiences wanted to see Elle succeed.

trivia.text: The screenwriters wrote a new ending taking place at graduation, which was filmed at Dulwich College in London, England since Witherspoon was in that city filming The Importance of Being Earnest.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip shows the new ending taking place at graduation, which was filmed at Dulwich College.

trivia.text: Witherspoon had also cut her hair for that film and Wilson had shaved his head for The Royal Tenenbaums.
trivia.match_reasoning: The clip features Reese Witherspoon and Luke Wilson, who had both altered their hair for other roles prior to filming this reshoot.

trivia.text: As both actors had changed their hair for their next movies, each had to wear wigs for the scene.
trivia.match_reasoning: Because they had changed their hair, both Reese Witherspoon and Luke Wilson are wearing wigs in this graduation scene.

scenes.summary: The end credits roll.

songs.title: Don't Need You To (Tell Me I'm Pretty)
